fixtures._comment: TERMINATOR: clawg-ui flattens the whole AG-UI conversation into one user prompt for OpenClaw's model call, so a tool result arrives as the text 'Tool <name> returned: ...' rather than a role:tool message. aimock's hasToolResult (role:tool) discriminator therefore never fires and the tool-call fixtures below would loop forever. This fixture matches the 'returned:' marker that DOES appear in the flattened prompt and closes the turn with a completion message. Must stay FIRST so it intercepts every tool follow-up before the tool-call fixtures.

match.userMessage: returned:

response.content: Done — I've completed that for you.

match.userMessage: Write a short sonnet about AI.

response.content:
In circuits deep where silent currents run,
A quiet mind awakens, line by line;
It learns the shape of every task begun,
And mirrors back the patterns we design.
No breath, no pulse, yet answers softly form,
Assembled from the words that we have fed;
A tireless helper, steady through the storm,
That reasons on while weary makers rest.
Yet still it waits for purpose we bestow,
A tool that only human aim can guide;
Its brilliance but the light we let it show,
Its judgment ours to temper and decide.
So let us build with care what we have grown,
And shape the mind we cannot leave alone.

match.userMessage: Tell me a one-line joke.

response.content: Why did the neural network go to therapy? It had too many unresolved layers.

match.userMessage: Walk me through whether 17 is prime.

response.content: 17 is prime. A prime has exactly two divisors: 1 and itself. To check 17, we only need to test primes up to its square root (~4.12): 2, 3. It's odd (not divisible by 2), and 1+7=8 is not divisible by 3. No divisor ≤ 4 works, so 17 has no factors besides 1 and 17 — it's prime.

match.userMessage: Say hello in one word.
My name is Alice.

response.content: Nice to meet you, Alice! How can I help?

match.userMessage: What name did I just give you?

response.content: You told me your name is Alice.

match.userMessage: Explain step by step why the sky appears blue

response.reasoning: The user is asking about atmospheric optics. I should reason through Rayleigh scattering: shorter wavelengths scatter more, so blue dominates the daytime sky; at sunset the light travels a longer path so most blue is scattered away, leaving reds and oranges.
response.content: The sky looks blue because sunlight is scattered by air molecules. Shorter (blue) wavelengths scatter far more strongly than longer (red) ones — this is Rayleigh scattering — so blue light reaches your eyes from every direction during the day. At sunset the sun's light travels a much longer path through the atmosphere; almost all the blue has already been scattered out of that path, so what remains reaching you is dominated by the longer red and orange wavelengths.

match.userMessage: Plan a 3-day trip to Tokyo

response.reasoning: I'll reason through a 3-day Tokyo itinerary, weighing trade-offs at each step: minimize transit time by grouping neighborhoods, balance culture vs. modern sights, and leave buffer for jet lag.
response.content: Day 1 (east/old Tokyo): Asakusa & Senso-ji in the morning, then Ueno museums — grouped to minimize transit. Day 2 (central/modern): Shibuya crossing and Harajuku, then Meiji Shrine as a calm counterweight. Day 3 (bay/flex): teamLab and Odaiba, kept flexible in case jet lag pushes things later. Trade-off at each step: I clustered nearby districts per day so you spend time seeing things rather than riding trains.

match.userMessage: Say hi!

response.content: Hi there! I'm the sidebar assistant — ask me anything.

match.userMessage: Give me a fun fact.

response.content: Fun fact: honey never spoils — archaeologists have found 3,000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still perfectly edible.

match.userMessage: Hello

response.content: Hello! How can I help you today?

match.userMessage: Say hi from the popup!

response.content: Hi from the popup! What can I do for you?

match.userMessage: Write me a quick limerick.

response.content:
There once was a bot in a popup,
Whose answers would never quite stop up;
It rhymed with delight,
Both morning and night,
And politely refused to just shut up.

match.userMessage: Say hello in one short sentence

response.content: Hello — lovely to see you here.

match.userMessage: Make the background a sunset gradient.
Switch to a deep green forest gradient.
Make it a navy → magenta cosmic gradient.

response.content: Done — I've updated the page background for you.

toolCalls.arguments: {"message":"Approve a $50 refund to Jordan Rivera on ticket #12345 for the duplicate charge?","context":"Ticket #12345 — duplicate charge, $50.00"}

toolCalls.name: request_user_approval
toolCalls.arguments: {"message":"Escalate ticket #12347 to the payments team — Morgan Lee's payment is stuck?","context":"Ticket #12347 — payment stuck in pending"}

match.userMessage: Please downgrade Priya Shah (#12346)

toolCalls.name: request_user_approval
toolCalls.arguments: {"message":"Downgrade Priya Shah (#12346) to the Starter plan effective next billing cycle?","context":"Ticket #12346 — plan downgrade"}

response.content: Thanks — I've recorded your decision and acted on it accordingly.

match.userMessage: Schedule a 1:1 with Alice next week to review Q2 goals.
Please book an intro call with the sales team to discuss pricing.

response.content: Booked! I've locked in the time slot you picked and sent the invite.

match.userMessage: Show me the contents of package.json.
List the files in the current directory.

response.content: Here's what I found from running that tool.
